_comment: AgentOS model registry — the single source of truth for which model does what. Code resolves its model from here (lucid_models.get(id)); the audit panel renders it. Edit here, not in code. All entries are LOCAL — nothing leaves this machine.

models.purpose: Looks at an opening image — uploaded or generated — to flag a real person (asks consent) or a possible minor (hard block) before it can be animated. The deterministic opencv face detector is the primary gate; this model is the real-vs-drawn / age second opinion.
models.notes: Small on purpose: it must load even when the GPU is busy, so the consent check never silently fails. opencv Haar (CPU) backstops it.

models.purpose: Proposes the 3–4 'what happens next' choices each turn, and expands the chosen one into a motion prompt. Output is schema-validated and red-line-filtered before it can reach the GPU.
models.notes: Evicted (keep_alive:0) before the video step so it never co-resides with the 24GB i2v model.

models.purpose: Renders the dream's opening frame from a typed description. The result still passes B2 (a t2i can render a person too).
models.notes: Fast (1–4 steps). The description passes the red-line gate first; generation is predict-before-load so it refuses rather than OOMs.

models.purpose: The actual dream: animates the current frame into the next clip, last-frame → first-frame, steered by the chosen beat.
models.notes: The heavy one. Runs only through the VRAM coordinator lease (Spawn/confirm-evict/Release); live inference always outranks it.
